mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.25% signal an emergency monetary easing regime that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset price multiples. This mechanism operates continuously when rates are near zero, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.19% as of 2016-02-15 indicates sustained near-zero policy
regime_hint: post-crisis zero lower bound monetary regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth decelerates below 100,000 net new jobs, signaling weakening labor demand and reduced consumer spending capacity. This triggers negative equity returns within 5 trading days as forward earnings expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 100,000 persons
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with decelerating job growth
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month by more than 1%, combined with housing starts (HOUST) falling below 500,000 annualized units, indicates simultaneous contraction in consumer and residential investment demand. This joint deterioration predicts negative SPX returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month decline > 1% AND HOUST < 500,000 units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 363,704 million and HOUST at 546,000 units as of 2016-02-15
regime_hint: demand-side recession or sharp cyclical slowdown
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (both conditions met) shows no statistically significant predictive power (Rank IC < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines for two consecutive months, signaling contraction in manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side weakness. This persistent supply deterioration predicts negative equity returns over 20 trading days as profit margins compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 20-day returns is below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, signaling accelerating labor market deterioration. This threshold crossing triggers negative SPX returns over 60 trading days as recession risk premia increase.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3-month rolling window
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession phase
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the correlation between 3-month UNRATE changes and 60-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS is held near zero, indicating the Fed maintains accommodative policy in a low-inflation environment. This combination sustains positive equity valuations over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.597 and FEDFUNDS at 0.19% as of 2016-02-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling weakening aggregate demand. This threshold crossing predicts negative SPX returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings growth expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,191.5 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2016-01-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 52% of observations when GDPC1 growth < 1.5%, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day returns is below 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 1% month-over-month while payroll employment (PAYEMS) simultaneously grows above 150,000 net new jobs, indicating synchronized supply and labor expansion. This joint acceleration predicts positive SPX returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 1% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change > 150,000 persons
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.2093 and PAYEMS at 130,311 thousand as of 2016-02-15
regime_hint: synchronized cyclical expansion with strong supply and labor dynamics
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (both conditions met) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
